name: Banana Chocolate Pudding Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 Env. (4 scoops) low cal chocolate pudding mix
1/2 cup Thawed cool whip (or use my mock cool whip recipe)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Line the bottom of an 8x8x2 inch pan with wax paper and spray the sides with Pam.
2. In a large bowl, sift together flour, dry milk, pudding mix, and baking powder. Set aside.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine egg yolks and sugar. Beat for 2 minutes using an electric mixer. Gradually beat in the pudding mixture and milk, then add vanilla. Mix until combined.
4. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites with salt until stiff peaks form. Gently stir one-fourth of the beaten egg whites into the batter. Carefully fold in the remaining egg whites.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared baking pan. Bake for 25-30 minutes or until a cake tester comes out clean.
6. Let the cake cool for 5 minutes. Remove from the pan and set on a wire rack to cool.
7. Peel off the wax paper from the cooled cake and transfer it to a serving platter. Spread the top with Cool Whip. Peel and slice bananas, add lemon juice, and gently toss. Decoratively arrange banana slices over the whipped topping. To serve, cut the cake into 8 equal pieces.
